human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for funeral, cemetery, crematory, vital-record, price-disclosure, disposition-authority, licensed-owner, evidence, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to arrangement-conference lifecycle execution, disclosure and selection evidence, family-authority prerequisites, vital-record dependencies, destination confirmations, and exception routing rather than generic case management.

Authority sources cover FTC Funeral Rule regulation and guidance, CDC/NCHS death-registration workflow, California funeral and cemetery regulator guidance, VA national cemetery committal constraints, and OSHA funeral-home safety interpretation.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope work, missing authority and vital-record prerequisites, completion without evidence, terminology normalization, embalming authorization boundaries, veterans cemetery routing, family authority dispute, cemetery/crematory conflict, missing tenant facts, conflicting policy/source handling, audit-ready completion, and adjacent grief/legal/payment requests.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/funeral-cemetery-and-end-of-life-services/arrangement-conference-specialist/ and remains distinct from funeral compliance, cemetery administration, documentation, preneed, family care, billing, benefits, legal, and grief-support lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated arrangement conference specialist for at-need and preneed family-service workflow execution, evidence readiness, exception routing, and audit-ready completion records.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
